atomicity.message: Staged payload is exactly lint Unit D: burpvalve lint init CLI and robot wiring in cmd/burpvalve, with tests; unrelated dirty worktree files are unstaged.

conditions.message: Unit D lint init CLI/robot wiring is formatted, hash-bound, and compatible with lint rule expectations.
evidence: ScarletMarsh verified staged hash sha256:ba7ccd5b43c440dd0c365245885174d2587a31951e75eb0bf4b25eaecf7ef770 and exact staged paths: cmd/burpvalve/lint_init.go, cmd/burpvalve/lint_init_test.go, cmd/burpvalve/main.go.
git diff --cached --check for the three payload files produced no output; gofmt -l on the staged-index payload produced no output.
Focused Unit D tests and go test ./... passed in a staged-index temp tree.

conditions.message: The implementation reuses existing lint detection/planning helpers and avoids duplicate manifest/rules mutation logic.
evidence: BrightOwl inspected buildLintInitResult/applyLintInitWrites and confirmed they use DetectLintSetup plus Plan/WriteLintManifestUpdate and Plan/WriteLintRulesRecommendationsUpdate.
BrightOwl verified shared conversion helpers keep CLI and robot paths on the same data model.
Staged-snapshot go test ./... passed.

conditions.message: The new lint init flow does not let agents claim enforced lint from a preview or unconfirmed setup.
evidence: BrightOwl verified detection is read-only, writes require explicit confirmation, robot mutation requires confirm=true, and output distinguishes proposed commands/coverage from enforcement.
Tests cover --detect never mutating, unconfirmed --write blocking, robots without confirm blocking, and robots confirm=true writing.
ScarletMarsh independently agreed this cell passes and found no superficial-success shortcut in the staged source/tests.

conditions.message: The governed Unit D behavior has targeted tests covering the new CLI and robot surfaces.
evidence: ScarletMarsh inspected staged lint_init_test.go and found tests for help flags, robot schemas, lint JSON/jobs compatibility, detect no-mutation, write confirmation, robot confirm, confirmed writes, and preset filtering.
Staged-index temp tree: go test ./cmd/burpvalve -run 'Test.*LintInit|Test.*LintJSONCompat|Test.*Robots|Test.*DetectNeverMutates|Test.*JobsFlag' -count=1 passed.

conditions.message: Unit D is complete: lint init exists, flags/help/robot docs are wired, compatibility is preserved, and jobs is validated without changing execution semantics.
evidence: BrightOwl verified the exact staged payload and hash-bound packet.
BrightOwl inspected main.go wiring for lint init, --jobs, robot input/output docs, and robot notes.
Focused Unit D tests and staged-snapshot go test ./... passed.

conditions.message: The evidence trail is explicit, independently generated, and bound to the current staged payload.
evidence: Packet supplied feature id, manifest hash, staged payload hash, and payload path list; ScarletMarsh independently verified all of them via burpvalve verifier prompts.
ScarletMarsh read staged files with git show :path, inspected git diff --cached, ran diff check, gofmt check, focused tests, and go test ./... in a staged-index temp tree.
BrightOwl supplied independent judgment-cell evidence in Agent Mail message 2678.

conditions.message: The staged slice is scoped to Unit D CLI/robot wiring; unrelated dirty worktree files are not in the index-bound payload.
evidence: BrightOwl verified git diff --cached --name-status contains only A cmd/burpvalve/lint_init.go, A cmd/burpvalve/lint_init_test.go, M cmd/burpvalve/main.go.
BrightOwl observed unrelated .beads, bcm, vorch, and presets files remain unstaged/untracked and outside staged-snapshot verification.
ScarletMarsh independently agreed scope-control passes for the same exact path list.

conditions.message: Mutation is bounded and confirmation-gated: lint init writes only Burpvalve manifest/recommendation artifacts after explicit confirmation.
evidence: BrightOwl inspected applyLintInitWrites and confirmed it calls only WriteLintManifestUpdate and WriteLintRulesRecommendationsUpdate after confirmation checks.
Tests assert --detect with --write/--force does not mutate, unconfirmed --write does not mutate, robots without confirm does not mutate, and robots confirm writes expected manifest commands.
No package manager, formatter config, CI, hook, shell script, or dependency installation mutation path is introduced.

conditions.message: Read-only and write paths preserve project data integrity and keep mutation targets bounded.
evidence: ScarletMarsh verified --detect returns planned updates without writing, --write requires force/robotConfirm/interactive confirmation, --detect overrides --write, and robot input uses strict decoding.
Write paths are limited to backpressure/manifest.yaml and backpressure/lint-rules.md through Unit C planner/write helpers.
Tests verify detect and unconfirmed write modes do not mutate, while confirmed robot write adds expected Go lint commands.

conditions.message: The CLI/robot surface preserves noninteractive security boundaries and does not execute detected lint commands during init.
evidence: BrightOwl verified robot input is structured, robot output documents exact fields, robot mode never prompts, and robot mutation requires confirm=true.
BrightOwl verified build/apply paths inspect repository files and plan/write Burpvalve manifest/rules updates without executing package-manager scripts or arbitrary detected commands.
ScarletMarsh independently agreed the staged payload has no secrets, auth changes, external API calls, network calls, sandbox escapes, or permission broadening.

conditions.message: The staged payload changes CLI command wiring, JSON/robot schemas, backend lint init behavior, and Go tests only; it does not alter browser UI, screenshots, canvas, responsive layout, or rendered visual assets.
evidence: ScarletMarsh verified the staged payload contains only cmd/burpvalve/main.go, cmd/burpvalve/lint_init.go, and cmd/burpvalve/lint_init_test.go and inspected their staged diff/content.

conditions.message: The new --jobs surface is validation/documentation only in this unit and does not introduce parallel execution or unbounded work.
evidence: ScarletMarsh inspected lint init detection/planning/write paths and verified --jobs is validated as positive metadata while execution remains sequential until the parallel increment.
Focused Unit D tests and go test ./... passed in a staged-index temp tree.

conditions.message: The agent-facing robot contract is explicit, noninteractive, and requires affirmative robot confirmation before mutation.
evidence: BrightOwl verified robotOutputDoc documents status, mutating, detection, coverage, proposed_commands, manifest_update, lint_rules_update, confirmation_required, warnings, and next_steps.
Tests verify robot help documents schemas, robot no-confirm blocks mutation, and confirm=true reports written/mutating.
